title: arctostaphylos alpina subsp. rubra
intro: Arctostaphylos alpina subsp. rubra, also known as Red Bearberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of arbutin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Red Bearberry include its potential to help alleviate urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Red Bearberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, making it a versatile addition to herbal remedies for various health concerns.
